author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LIII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this Psalm we undertake to treat with you, as far as the Lord supplieth us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A brother biddeth us that we may have the will, and prayeth that we may have the power.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If anything in haste perchance I shall have passed over, He that even to us deigneth to give what we shall be enabled to say, will supply it in you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of it is: “At the end, for Maeleth, understanding to David himself.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For Maeleth,” as we find in interpretations of Hebrew names, seemeth to say, For one travailing, or in pain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who there is in this world that travaileth and is in pain, the faithful acknowledge, because thereof they are.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Christ here travaileth, Christ here is in pain: the Head is above, the members below.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For one not travailing nor in pain would not say, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Him, with whom when persecuting He was travailing, being converted, He made to travail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he also was himself afterwards enlightened, and grafted on those members which he used to persecute; being pregnant with the same love, he said, “My little children, of whom again I travail, until Christ be formed in you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the members therefore of Christ, for His Body which is the Church, for that same One Man, that is, for that very unity, whereof the Head is above, this Psalm is sung.…Who are they, then, amid whom we travail and groan, if in the Body of Christ we are, if under Him, the Head, we live, if amongst His members we are counted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who they are, hear ye.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The unwise man hath said in his heart, There is no God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such sort is it of men amid whom is pained and groaneth the Body of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If such is this sort of men, of not many do we travail; as far as seemeth to occur to our thoughts, very few there are; and a difficult thing it is to meet with a man that saith in his heart, “There is no God;” nevertheless, so few there are, that, fearing amid the many to say this, in their heart they say it, for that with mouth to say it they dare not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not much then is that which we are bid to endure, hardly is it found: uncommon is that sort of men that say in their heart, “There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, if it be examined in another sense, is not that found to be in more men, which we supposed to be in men few and uncommon, and almost in none?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them come forth into the midst that live evil lives, let us look into the doings of profligate, daring, and wicked men, of whom there is a great multitude; who foster day by day their sins, who, their acts having been changed into habit, have even lost sense of shame: this is so great a multitude of men, that the Body of Christ, set amid them, scarce dareth to censure that which it is not constrained to commit, and deemeth it a great matter for itself that the integrity of innocence be preserved in not doing that which now, by habit, either it doth not dare to blame, or if it shall have dared, there breaketh out the censure and recrimination of them that live evil lives, more readily than the free voice of them that live good lives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And those men are such as say in their heart, “There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such men I am confuting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence confuting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That their doings please God, they judge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He doth not therefore affirm, “some say,” but “The unwise man hath said in his heart, There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Which men do so far believe there is a God, that the same God they judge with what they do to be pleased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if thou being wise dost perceive, how “the unwise man hath said in his heart, There is no God,” if thou give heed, if thou understand, if thou examine; he that thinketh that evil doings please God, Him he doth not think to be God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if God is, He is just; if He is just, injustice displeaseth Him, iniquity displeaseth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou, when thou thinkest that iniquity pleaseth Him, dost deny God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if God is one Whom iniquity displeaseth, but God seemeth not to thee to be one whom iniquity displeaseth, and there is no God but one whom iniquity displeaseth, then when thou sayest in thy heart, God doth countenance my iniquities, thou sayest nothing else than, “There is no God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us advert also to that sense, which concerning Christ our Lord Himself, our Head Himself, doth present itself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when Himself in form of a servant appeared on earth, they that crucified Him said, “He is not God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because Son of God He was, truly God He was.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they that are corrupted and have become abominable said what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He is not God:” let us slay Him, “He is not God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast the voice of these very men in the book of Wisdom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For after there had gone before the verse, “The unwise man hath said in his heart, There is no God;” as if reasons were required why the unwise man could say this, he hath subjoined, “Corrupted they are, and abominable have become in their iniquities” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear ye those corrupted men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For they have said with themselves, not rightly thinking:” corruption beginneth with evil belief, thence it proceedeth to depraved morals, thence to the most flagrant iniquities, these are the grades.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what with themselves said they, thinking not rightly?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “A small thing and with tediousness is our life.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From this evil belief followeth that which also the Apostle hath spoken of, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we shall die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in the former passage more diffusely luxury itself is described: “Let us crown us with roses, before they be withered; in every place let us leave the tokens of our gladness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After the more diffuse description of that luxury, what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let us slay the poor just man:” this is therefore saying, “He is not God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Soft words they seemed but now to say: “Let us crown us with roses, before they be withered.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What more delicate, what more soft?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou expect, out of this softness, Crosses, swords?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wonder not, soft are even the roots of brambles; if any one handle them, he is not pricked: but that wherewith thou shalt be pricked from thence hath birth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Corrupted,” therefore, are those men, “and abominable have become in their iniquities.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They say, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold them openly saying, “He is not God.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The Lord from Heaven hath looked forth upon the sons of men, that He might see if there is one understanding and seeking after God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Corrupted they are,” all these that say, “There is no God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did it escape God, that they were become such?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Or indeed to us would their inward thought be opened, except by Him it were told?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then He understood, if then He knew, what is this which hath been said, “that He might see”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the words are of one inquiring, of one not knowing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God from Heaven hath looked forth,” etc. And as though He had found what He sought by looking upon, and by looking down from Heaven, He giveth sentence: “All men have gone aside, together useless they have become: there is not one that doeth good, not so much as one” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two questions arise somewhat difficult: for if God looketh out from Heaven, in order that He may see if there is one understanding or seeking after God; there stealeth upon an unwise man the thought, that God knoweth not all things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is one question: what is the other?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there is not one that doeth good, is not so much as one; who is he that travaileth amid bad men?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former question then is solved as followeth: ofttimes the Scripture speaketh in such manner, that what by the gift of God a creature doth, God is said to do.…For hence has been said the following also, “For the Spirit searcheth all things, even the depth of God;” not because He that knoweth all things searcheth, but because to thee hath been given the Spirit, which maketh thee also to search: and that which by His own gift thou doest, He is said to do; because without Him thou wouldest not do it: therefore God is said to do, when thou doest.…And because this by the gift of God thou doest, God from heaven is “looking forth upon the sons of men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former question then, according to our measure, thus hath been solved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that which looking forth we acknowledge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is that which looking forth God acknowledgeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What (because here He giveth it) doth He acknowledge?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear what it is; that “All have gone aside, together useless they have become: there is not one that doeth good, there is not so much as one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is that other question, but the same whereof a little before I have made mention?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If, “There is not one that doeth good, is not so much as one,” no one remaineth to groan amid evil men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Stay, saith the Lord, do not hastily give judgment.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have given to men to do well; but of Me, He saith, not of themselves: for of themselves evil they are: sons of men they are, when they do evil; when well, My sons.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this thing God doth, out of sons of men He maketh sons of God: because out of Son of God He hath made Son of Man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what this participation is: there hath been promised to us a participation of Divinity: He lieth that hath promised, if He is not first made partaker of mortality.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Son of God hath been made partaker of mortality, in order that mortal man may be made partaker of divinity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that hath promised that His good is to be shared with thee, first with thee hath shared thy evil: He that to thee hath promised divinity, showeth in thee love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore take away that men are sons of God, there remaineth that they are sons of men: “There is none that doeth good, is not so much as one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not all know that work iniquity, that devour My people for the food of bread”? (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5).…There is therefore here a people of God that is being devoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, “There is not one that doeth good, there is not so much as one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We reply by the rule above.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this people that is devoured, this people that suffereth evil men, this that groaneth and travaileth amid evil men, now out of sons of men have been made sons of God: therefore are they devoured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “The counsel of the needy man thou hast confounded, because the Lord is his hope.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ofttimes, in order that the people of God may be devoured, this very thing in it is despised, that it is the people of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will pillage, he saith, and despoil; if he is a Christian, what will he do to me?…But what followeth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will convince thee, and will set thee before thy face.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not now know so as thou shouldest be displeasing to thyself, thou shalt know so as thou mayest mourn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For God cannot but show to the unrighteous their iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If He is not to show, who will they be that are to say, “What hath profited us pride, and what hath boasting of riches bestowed upon us?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For then shall they know, that now will not know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Shall not all know?” etc. Why hath He added, “for the food of bread”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it were as bread, they eat My people.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all other things which we eat, we can eat now these, now those; not always this vegetable, not always this flesh, not always these apples: but always bread.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is then, “Devour My people for the food of bread”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without intermission, without cessation they devour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “On God they have not called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He is comforting the man that groaneth, and chiefly by an admonition, lest by imitating evil men, who ofttimes prosper, they delight in evil doing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is kept for thee that which to thee hath been promised: their hope is present, thine is future, but theirs is transient, thine sure; theirs false, thine true.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they “upon God have not called.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not daily such men ask of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They do “not” ask of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Give heed, if I am able to say this by the aid of God Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God gratuitously will have Himself to be worshipped, gratuitously will have Himself to be loved, that is chastely to be loved; not Himself to be loved for the reason that He giveth anything besides Himself, but because He giveth Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then that calleth upon God in order that He may be made rich, on God doth not call: for upon that He calleth which to himself he willeth to come.…But now thou wouldest have coffer full, and conscience void: God filleth not coffer, but breast.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What do outward riches profit thee, if inward need presseth thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore those men that for the sake of worldly comforts, that for the sake of earthly good things, that for the sake of present life and earthly felicity, call upon God, do not call upon God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this reason what followeth concerning them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “There have they feared with fear, where there was no fear” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For is there fear, if a man lose riches?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is no fear there, and yet in that case men are afraid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if a man lose wisdom, truly there is fear, and in that case he is not afraid.…Thou hast feared to give back money, and hast willed to lose fidelity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Martyrs took not away property of other persons, but even their own they despised that they might not lose fidelity: and it was too little to lose money, when they were proscribed; they took also their life when they suffered: they lost life, in order that unto everlasting life they might find it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore there they feared, where they ought to have been afraid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they that of Christ have said, “He is not God,” have there feared where was no fear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they said, “If we shall have let Him go, there will come the Romans, and will take away from us both place and kingdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O folly and imprudence saying in its heart, “He is not God”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast feared to lose earth, thou hast lost Heaven: thou hast feared lest there should come the Romans, and take away from thee place and kingdom!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Could they take away from thee God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then remaineth? what but that thou confess, that thou hast willed to keep, and by keeping ill hast lost?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou hast lost both place and nation by slaying Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For ye did will rather to slay Christ, than to lose place; and ye have lost place, and nation, and Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fearing, they have slain Christ: but wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For God hath scattered the bones of them that please men.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Willing to please men, they feared to lose their place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Christ Himself, of whom they said, “He is not God,” willed rather to displease such men, as they were: sons of men, not sons of God, He willed rather to displease.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence were scattered their bones, His bones no one hath broken.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They were confounded, for God hath despised them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In very deed, brethren, as far as regardeth them, great confusion hath come to them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the place where they crucified the Lord, whom for this cause they crucified, that they might not lose both place and nation, the Jews are not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “God,” therefore, “hath despised them:” and yet in despising He warned them to be converted.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them now confess Christ, and say, He is God, of whom they said, “He is not God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them return to the inheritance of their fathers, to the inheritance of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, let them possess with these very persons life eternal: though they have lost life temporal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because out of sons of men have been made sons of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so long as they remain, and will not, there is not one that doeth good, there is not so much as one.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They were confounded, for God hath despised them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as though to these very persons He were turned, He saith, “Who shall give out of Sion salvation to Israel?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: O ye fools, ye revile, insult, buffet, besmear with spittings, with thorns ye crown, upon the Cross ye lift up; whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Who shall give out of Sion salvation to Israel?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall not That Same of whom ye have said, “He is not God”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In God’s turning away the captivity of His people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For there turneth away the captivity of His people, no one but He that hath willed to be a captive in your own hands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what men shall understand this thing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jacob shall exult, and Israel shall rejoice.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Israel;” the true Jacob, and the true Israel, that younger, to whom the elder was servant, shall himself exult, for he shall himself understand.
